disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Acute physical collapse over the last 48 hours: unable to stay awake more than about two hours, unable to get out of bed, whole-body exhaustion, on a background of six months of worsening tiredness
trigger_question_examples: What has changed in the last day or two?
How bad has the tiredness got?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The last two days I've just crashed. I can't stay awake more than a couple of hours and I can't get out of bed. It's been building for months but this is a whole other level.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Frequent recent viral-type illnesses / catching colds repeatedly over recent weeks
trigger_question_examples: Have you been unwell or picking up infections lately?
Any fevers, sore throat or swollen glands?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, I seem to catch every bug going around at the moment. No high fevers that I've noticed, just constantly run down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe sleep disturbance — barely sleeping despite profound exhaustion
trigger_question_examples: Are you actually able to sleep when you lie down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: That's the strange part — I'm shattered but I can't actually sleep properly. I'm running on empty.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sensation of the heart racing / pounding at times (patient-reported palpitation)
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed your heart racing or pounding?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, yeah, sometimes my heart feels like it's going really fast even when I'm just lying there.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Very high caffeine intake — six or more cups of coffee daily to force wakefulness
trigger_question_examples: How much caffeine or coffee are you having?
Are you using anything to keep yourself awake?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Loads of coffee — six, seven cups a day easily, just to keep going.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Low mood and loss of enjoyment are confined to work; the patient still finds pleasure and interest in life outside of work
trigger_question_examples: Outside of work, are you still able to enjoy things?
Is the low feeling there all the time, or mainly around work?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly it's all about work. When I'm away from it, on a good day, I can still enjoy my friends and my own stuff — it's the job that's crushing me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No current thoughts of self-harm or suicide when sensitively screened; the patient can contract for safety
trigger_question_examples: When people are this exhausted and overwhelmed, they sometimes have thoughts of not wanting to go on or of hurting themselves — has anything like that crossed your mind?
Have you had any thoughts of harming yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that. I don't want to hurt myself — I just desperately want it to stop and to be able to rest.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: That's a heavy question... no. I'm exhausted, not suicidal. I just need this to ease off.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain and no actual fainting; reports only mild lightheadedness when getting up quickly, without collapse
trigger_question_examples: Any chest pain, or have you actually fainted or blacked out?
When you stand up, do you feel like you might pass out?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain, and I haven't actually fainted. I get a bit lightheaded if I stand up fast but it passes.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms are strongly patterned to occupational demands and an unsustainable, unrecognised extra workload over months
trigger_question_examples: What do you think has driven this?
What has your workload been like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been doing so much extra work that nobody credits — committees, mentoring, holding everyone else together — on top of my actual job, for months. It's never let up.
